rationale: The two papers are tightly aligned on core constructs: the citing paper develops a confusion metric for visual copycat packages, while the cited paper studies how copycat–leader similarity (including package design) affects outcomes under different evaluation modes. The citing note explicitly says it extends prior copycat/trademark-dilution research, and this cited JMR article is a central prior result in that exact area (confirmed by DOI match), so this is not an obvious incidental mention.
